response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said's career is built almost entirely on web and application development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Ember.js), with no meaningful C++ experience — the role's single most critical hard requirement is 8 years of C++. His skills section omits C++ entirely, and the only traceable exposure is a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015. Beyond that, the role requires 5+ years in large-scale cloud infrastructure or compute platforms, which is also absent; his infrastructure exposure is limited to Kubernetes setup for an OSS side project and AWS CDK for a direct-mail pricing service — far below the seniority level needed for a cloud compute platform technical lead.
strengths: Demonstrated engineering craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and a track record of teaching and running workshops
Experience operating at high throughput at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O), showing comfort with performance-sensitive systems
Breadth of seniority across multiple staff-level roles and a founder track record, indicating ownership and initiative
concerns: C++ experience is effectively non-existent relative to the 8-year minimum; the language does not appear in the candidate's skills list and the only exposure was a 6-month junior role in 2014
No cloud compute platform, hyperscaler infrastructure, or distributed systems experience at the scale or depth the role requires
No formal technical leadership title or record of leading engineering strategy, roadmap ownership, or cross-team architectural alignment
